intro.kicker: Topic 1 of 8 • Chapter: Surface Tension • Unit: Properties of Matter • Physics
intro.description: The force of attraction between molecules of the same substance. This force is lesser in liquids and least in gases. This topic covers Cohesive Force and Adhesive Force. In NEET Physics, Intermolecular Force is tested directly and as part of multi-concept problems. Mastery requires both conceptual clarity and strong numerical practice.

expectedQuestions.description: Intermolecular Force features in NEET Physics as a direct question or embedded in multi-step problems. Detailed study of Surface Tension is recommended.

timeRequired.description: Theory 10–15 min, plus solving 20 practice problems. Efficient if you know the formula derivation.

difficulty.description: Surface Tension difficulty level. Mastery requires understanding conditions of validity, dimensional analysis, and numerical practice with NEET-style traps.

nriUsaGap.description: Intermolecular Force under Properties of Matter receives minimal AP Physics coverage. NEET dedicates significant weightage here. NRI/US-curriculum students must study this chapter in full.

bulletNotes: Intermolecular Force appears regularly in NEET Physics; never skip it.
Often combined with adjacent topics in multi-step problems.
Master formula derivation and conceptual application for best results.

strategySteps.title: Concept First
strategySteps.description: Understand the physical meaning behind Intermolecular Force. Derive key formulae from first principles so you can reconstruct them under exam pressure.

strategySteps.title: Formula Mapping
strategySteps.description: List all formulae for this topic on a single sheet. Note the conditions under which each applies. NEET frequently tests boundary conditions and exceptions.

strategySteps.title: Practice NEET-style MCQs
strategySteps.description: Solve at least 20 past-paper-style questions on this topic. Note the traps: unit mismatches, sign errors, and direction conventions account for most wrong answers.

cards.title: Core Concept: Intermolecular Force
cards.coreRevision: Intermolecular Force is a key pillar of Surface Tension in NEET Physics. Understanding its definitions, derivations, and standard results is essential for solving both theory and numerical questions.
points: Intermolecular Force is a fundamental concept in Surface Tension
Always verify units and dimensional consistency
cards.example: Formula: Cohesive or adhesive forces are inversely proportional to the eighth power of distance between molecules

cards.title: NEET Traps to Avoid
cards.coreRevision: NEET setters target conceptual gaps in Intermolecular Force. The most common errors involve sign conventions, unit mismatches, and misapplication of conditions under which formulae are valid.
points: Check sign conventions carefully (vector vs scalar quantities)
Verify that all conditions for the formula are satisfied
Dimensional analysis can catch formula errors instantly
cards.example: In NEET, Intermolecular Force questions often combine with adjacent chapter concepts. Practice multi-step problems to expose hidden links.

curriculum.hintText: Topics in Intermolecular Force covered differently or not at all in AP Physics vs NEET.

gaps.title: Intermolecular Force in US vs Indian Curriculum
gaps.description: Properties of matter (elasticity, surface tension, fluid mechanics) receive minimal coverage in AP Physics 1. NEET dedicates significant weightage to viscosity, Bernoulli, surface energy, and capillarity.
bullets: NEET expects derivation-level mastery; AP Physics often treats Intermolecular Force as applied only.
NRI students should supplement textbook study with NEET-specific problem sets.
Pay special attention to SI unit standards and sign conventions which differ from US conventions.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes Intermolecular Force in the context of NEET Physics?

options.text: Intermolecular Force applies only under idealized conditions with no friction or air resistance
Intermolecular Force is a fundamental result derivable from first principles and applies within well-defined physical conditions
Intermolecular Force has no mathematical formulation and is purely qualitative
Intermolecular Force contradicts Newton's laws in non-inertial frames

questions.explanation: Intermolecular Force is a rigorously derived physical principle within Surface Tension. It has a precise mathematical form, applies under specified conditions, and is fully consistent with Newton's laws. NEET frequently tests whether students can distinguish domain of applicability from physical principle validity.

questions.question: In the study of Surface Tension, which statement about Adhesive Force is CORRECT for a NEET Physics student?

options.text: It is only tested as an isolated topic and never combined with energy or momentum problems
The dimensional formula does not follow from SI base units
It is integral to Surface Tension and appears in direct and multi-concept NEET questions
It is outside the NEET Physics syllabus since 2020

questions.explanation: Adhesive Force is a core component of Surface Tension and regularly appears in NEET. Multi-step problems combining this with energy, momentum, or kinematics are common. Dimensional analysis confirms SI unit consistency. This topic has remained in the NEET syllabus continuously.

questions.question: In a NEET context, when is Intermolecular Force most directly applicable?
options: Only in ideal, frictionless, zero-gravity scenarios
Whenever the stated conditions for Intermolecular Force are satisfied, including in standard NEET problems
questions.answer: Whenever the stated conditions for Intermolecular Force are satisfied, including in standard NEET problems

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the dimensional formula of the primary physical quantity in Intermolecular Force?
options: It follows from SI base units with standard MLT notation
It requires non-SI units for proper expression
Dimensional analysis is inapplicable to this quantity
questions.answer: It follows from SI base units with standard MLT notation

questions.question: The most common NEET trap in Intermolecular Force problems is:
options: Applying the formula outside its domain of validity or with incorrect sign conventions
questions.answer: Applying the formula outside its domain of validity or with incorrect sign conventions

items.title: Define Intermolecular Force precisely
items.description: Write out the complete definition of Intermolecular Force including all qualifying conditions and the physical quantities involved.

items.title: Derive the key formula from first principles
items.description: Reproduce the main derivation without notes. Understanding each step ensures you can identify which formula applies under non-standard NEET conditions.

items.title: State conditions of validity
items.description: List all assumptions and conditions under which Intermolecular Force results hold. NEET setters test these boundaries.

items.title: Master Cohesive Force
items.description: Solve at least 5 NEET-style problems specifically on Cohesive Force to ensure pattern recognition and speed.

items.title: Master Adhesive Force
items.description: Solve at least 5 NEET-style problems specifically on Adhesive Force to ensure pattern recognition and speed.

items.question: How many questions from Intermolecular Force appear in NEET each year?
items.answer: Intermolecular Force typically contributes 0–2 questions per NEET paper depending on the year. While it may not appear every year, it often features in multi-concept problems linked to adjacent topics in Surface Tension. Skipping it is risky.

items.question: Is Intermolecular Force in the NEET 2025 syllabus?
items.answer: Yes, Intermolecular Force remains part of the NEET Physics syllabus under Surface Tension. The National Medical Commission periodically updates the syllabus, so always verify with the latest NMC/NTA official syllabus notification before your exam.

items.question: What is the best way to revise Intermolecular Force before NEET?
items.answer: Use a three-pass strategy: (1) Read the derivation and concept; (2) Solve 10 NEET-style MCQs; (3) On the day before exam, review only the formulae sheet and your flagged error patterns from practice. Focus on conditions of validity and sign conventions.

items.question: What is the most common mistake in Intermolecular Force problems?
items.answer: The most frequent errors in Intermolecular Force are: applying the formula outside its domain (e.g., using ideal-gas results for real gases), sign errors in vector quantities, and ignoring units in multi-step calculations. Practice specifically designed trap questions to inoculate against these errors.

grounding_excerpt: The force of attraction or repulsion acting between the molecules are known as intermolecular force. The nature of intermolecular force is electromagnetic.
The intermolecular forces of attraction may be classified into two types.
(ii) It is difficult to separate two sticky plates of glass welded with water.
(ii) A piece of paper sticks to another due to large force of adhesion between the paper and gum molecules.
